    CAGEMATCH Â» Wrestlers Database Â» Rusev

    Rusev

    Also known as Alexander Rusev, Miro, Miroslav Makaraov, Miroslav The Bulgarian, Bashing Bulgarian

    • Overview
    • News and Rumors
    • Articles
    • Career
    • Career highlights
    • Titles
    • Matches
    • Match Statistics

    General Data
    Current gimmick:
    Rusev
    Age:
    40 years
    Promotion:
    World Wrestling Entertainment
    Brand:
    RAW
    Active Roles:
    Singles Wrestler
    Personal Data
    Birthday:
    25.12.1985
    Birthplace:
    Plovdiv, Bulgarien
    Gender:
    male
    Height:
    6' 0" (183 cm)
    Weight:
    279 lbs (127 kg)
    Background in sports:
    Kraftdreikampf, Rudern
    WWW:
    •  https://x.com/ToBeMiro
    •  https://www.instagram.com/tobemiro
    •  https://www.twitch.tv/mirotwch
    Career Data
    Alter egos:
    Alexander Rusev
        a.k.a.  Rusev
    Bashing Bulgarian
    Miro
        a.k.a.  Miroslav Makaraov
        a.k.a.  Miroslav The Bulgarian
    Roles:
    Singles Wrestler (2008 - today)
    Tag Team Wrestler (2017 - 2018)
    WWE Development Wrestler (2010 - 2014)
    Beginning of in-ring career:
    22.11.2008
    In-ring experience:
    17 years
    Wrestling style:
    Powerhouse
    Trainer:
    Gangrel & Rikishi
    Nicknames:
    "God's Favourite Champion"
    "The Best Man"
    "The Bulgarian Brute"
    "The Redeemer"
    "The Russian Gladiator"
    Signature moves:
    Accolade (Camel Clutch)
    Game Over
    Machka Kick
    7.â28

    Average rating in 2020: 6.96  [27]
    Average rating in 2019: 7.48  [21]

    • Details & History
    • Ratings System Rules
    • Help and formula
    Latest comments of the CAGEMATCH inmates
    AJ Styles was better than your favorite wrote on 07.05.2026:
    [6.0] "He can have good performances at times but he's sort of bland to me. I haven't been a fan of him since the whole Rusev day stuff. His run as the redeemer was OK. Outside of his matches with Sheamus, I haven't been too high on his performances at all but he's not bad."
    SnowBunnyAlert45 wrote on 30.04.2026:
    [5.0] "Never liked the bloke. Foreign heels rarely appeal to me, unless it is in an intriguing way (see: Bret Hart, MJF outside the US), His early WWE run was nothing to write home about, his AEW run had some good matches, and his return to WWE sees him bottom of the peking order, getting an International title shot because of a Youtube show. His runs always felt flat, never really intriguing me as far as it goes. He only got limelight in his 1st WWE run because ol' Vince wanted to cash in on real world events, and used the foreign heel vs. national hero baby face as a way to sell tickets. Horribly mid wrestler, should stick to Twitch. edit: His stuff in WWE now looks good, hopefully now he can actually get good matches and storylines, +1 to the rating"
    shawnwrestling wrote on 19.02.2026:
    [8.0] "When Rusev first started, he was a very believable heel, dominating opponents and looking unstoppable. His feud with Ziggler marked the beginning of his downfall, but even then, he remained a solid in-ring worker. He consistently got over with the crowd on his own, despite WWE not always pushing him properly. Now he often jobs to other wrestlers, but his skill, intensity, and presence in the ring still make him a great performer. He's one of those guys who can make matches look real and exciting no matter the circumstances."
    StringsRollins wrote on 13.02.2026:
    [5.0] "Perfectly decent professional wrestler that needs a reality check on how much of a star he actually is. Confidence is one thing, being delusional about your importance to the wrestling world is another thing entirely. Not being willing to take losses- something even THE ROCK was doing back in the height of his stardom in the Attitude Era- while also not putting in enough good work to justify why you shouldn't be losing is a lethal combination to make me lose respect for you as a wrestler and person. It's unfortunate, really, because while I never saw him as a main event talent I always thought he did have something. Barring some sort of massive late-career reinvention I think a six is his ceiling. Rusev Day stuff was memorable and fun at least, though much of that is due to Aiden English being very talented on the mic."
    kacy noah wrote on 13.02.2026:
    [5.0] "Rusev sure is a guy. In all fairness, his feud with Sheamus was peak mid-card entertainment, and his brief feud with Dominik Mysterio was fun too. His character work is 100% there, but I'm frequently not impressed by his overall work. He can have some great matches, but he's not an in-ring beast. He can cut some entertaining promos and work some entertaining angles, but he's not a standout character."
    nWo-Joshi-Guy wrote on 03.02.2026:
    [5.0] "Best character work was obviously the redeemer character. Seemingly difficult to work with. Occasionally impressive in the ring but much of his work is generic or below average. Part of one of the worst storylines in WWE history, even if he was the best part of it."
    HammertonWay wrote on 31.07.2025:
    [4.0] "I remember near the end of his original WWE run really getting behind Rusev and getting upset with how he was getting treated. When he came over to AEW I thought he was mismanaged at first but was excited when started his "redeemer" gimmick. But then the issues came again and he wasn't getting TV time and I was confused by it all. The recent commentary that came to light after his release from AEW has left me bitter about him though. Not wanting to work, rather streaming online, not even wanting to put anyone else over before leaving. We've seen ex WWE guys leave AEW to go back, but they left on much better terms than he did, and what does he have to show for it? He can't get a reaction from the crowd to save his life now!"
    KENTAfan wrote on 05.07.2025:
    [9.0] "Rusev/Miro is one of the best powerhouse-style wrestlers of this generation, and I'll stand on that. His Mania entrance riding a fricking tank is one of the best ever, but aside from being flashy at times, Rusev is always a unit in the ring. His style in general isn't anything crazy, but his physicality and bruising style always makes for entertaining matches. I think my biggest criticism would be that he seems to experience periods where he lacks motivation, or his heart isn't fully in it, but when Rusev actually gets going he's awesome. I also can't blame him for being unmotivated at times, because both WWE and AEW wasted the majority of his potential in the past. But Rusev is still a beast, and even now he could absolutely have a few big main event level runs if WWE just finally decides to pull the trigger (hoping they do, because that actually sounds awesome)"
    SkyIcons wrote on 18.06.2025:
    [5.0] "Always thought this guy was just mid, that didn't change in AEW and that hasn't changed since his return to WWE. Solid big man wrestler, not much else. Guess he's gotten better on the mic with the redeemer gimmick but I still don't really like his promos."
    bherbert1980 wrote on 30.05.2025:
    [7.0] "An absolute powerhouse with surprising agility and great comedic timing when needed. His early WWE run as the unstoppable Bulgarian brute with Lana by his side was fantastic, and "Rusev Day" became an organic phenomenon that WWE never fully capitalized on. Strong in-ring presence, legit aura, and could've been an even bigger star with better booking. Still a dependable hand wherever he works"
    Raditz Flair wrote on 28.05.2025:
    [5.0] "A good big man and very good wrestler who can't get out of his own way. If any of the stories are accurate he seems to take himself way too seriously when it comes to getting pinned. Now he's back where he started. He's done nothing worth watching since he returned to WWE in 2025."
    Luna100 wrote on 24.05.2025:
    [4.0] "A wasted career. He has had his moments, but he has destroyed all good will with me through his whining online, and loads of stupid moments, the biggest waste of a contract in AEW, and stupid gimmicks in his later WWE run. Maybe this harsh, but he just annoys me."
    2pt0EcoBoostFusion wrote on 16.04.2025:
    [7.0] "Miro is never someone who is going to give you the best match ever, but he is a very reliable worker and has been poorly utilized in his career wherever he was. In WWE as Rusev, he had a kick-ass Russian gimmick and his WM 31 entrance with the Russian Anthem and Tank will go down in the wrestling entrances Hall of Fame, Even as an American myself I thought that was damn cool even if the Russian Federation was full of controversy at the time when the entrance happened, it was truly cool to see. Of course, Super Cena had to beat him for the US title and ruin his momentum and unfortunately, he never got his career back on track despite getting massively over as the Bulgarian Brute (I assume the character formerly being affiliated with Russia was too much controversy for WWE to bear) with the 'Happy Rusev Day' gimmick back in 2018, a gimmick that was a breath of fresh air compared to the DUDs that WWE was putting on TV in that putrid era. WWE of course dropped the ball with him which was just insane and I was hoping AEW wouldn't do the same either, but they couldn't do anything with him either and he it seems like his career is going to be forgotten by most wrestling fans, which is just crazy considering he is a good in-ring worker and an even better character. He is thankfully going back to WWE though and hopefully we get to see Rusev Day come back to the forefront of gimmicks and see that push he has deserved for so long come to fruition, I'm rooting for Rusev!"
    Beneyriey wrote on 23.02.2025:
    [7.0] "Miro's career has never truly taken off, largely due to poor booking--through no fault of his own. In WWE, they had the perfect chance to establish him as a dominant heel, only to squander it by having John Cena defeat him at WrestleMania. 'Rusev Day' had huge potential, yet WWE failed to capitalize on it. The same pattern has followed him to AEW--just as he starts building momentum, he disappears from TV. It's a shame, as he hasn't had a sustained run in years despite his talent."
    AceHagann wrote on 27.11.2024:
    [6.0] "An average wrestler. Not particularly gifted in the ring, lacking charisma and personality in the mic. Not very great under any category. He has had his moments, but those are few and far between."
    TreyHalo wrote on 26.10.2024:
    [6.0] "Miro always felt like a good midcard hand and not much more for me, his work never really moved me all that much post-2015 outside of a few exceptions in AEW, but I've always thought him to be solid enough, just not really worth the hype that some portray him as and overall, I'd say around the 6 range..."
    kaszi2K wrote on 31.08.2024:
    [6.0] "Miro seems destined for the midcard, while he does possess the look of a main eventer his lack of consistency both in terms of his in-ring performance and overall character direction in AEW and WWE along with apparent attitude issues leave much to be desired for the Redeemer."
    ismarizvi wrote on 22.05.2024:
    [7.0] "One of most strangest book person in the wrestling one time is at top of game but other time he is just squashing a jobber.But Miro/Rusev keep himself a solid no matter what situation he is in"
    metagodzilla wrote on 17.05.2024:
    [6.0] "Man what happened to this guy? He's always someone I wanted to like more than I could because in ring he just doesn't really have it. He had a pretty bad match with Bryan Danielson on AEW PPV and that's pretty big mark against you in my opinion. His gimmicks are fun when they're not trying to be funny."
    danzitorock wrote on 13.05.2024:
    [6.0] "He's a good powerhouse, better than a lot of people, his gimmicks as Rusev in WWE were pathetic several times, the guy used to go out there as a cartoon Russian villain with a Bulgarian tattoo on his arm, I don't think they ever used him properly, but then when he had the chance to do something cool in AEW, he chose to act like Hulk Hogan or something like that, but without having any excuse to back all this arrogance. It's a shame, because I truly think he's talented and has the potential to be one of the coolest monsters in television."
    TripleCrown wrote on 04.04.2024:
    [4.0] "I just don't get it. Miro is nothing special. People thought he was going to excel in AEW and the guy has done anything but that. His run in NXT was pretty bad, I still remember that piece of wood he would carry to the ring... pretty stupid, especially in the 2010s. Made a great impact in the 2016 Royal Rumble, his look really differentiated him from everybody else on the roster around that time. However, he just isn't capable of having good matches, he can talk which is a plus but everything else just falls short. His in-ring style is very bad and not suited for mainstream wrestling, guys like Walter/Gunther are able to do it because they just have that little something extra about them, whereas Miro just falls very flat."
    ultravioletshiroi wrote on 10.03.2024:
    [8.0] "Good wrestler, surprisingly agile for his size. He has surprisingly decent promo skills and has some charisma. Unfortunately he's been under utilized in AEW."
    hamstermatch wrote on 03.01.2024:
    [5.0] "He can go in the ring and used to have a fun gimmick for a while but sadly its not as it used to be. Its 2023, why would a man still choose to do a misogynist gimmick? Im not familiar with his work pre-AEW so the fans online clamouring for more of this guy is confusing and sours me on him. He really needs to sit and think about his character work and do some rewriting."
    Jon999 wrote on 22.11.2023:
    "Why does it seem like you only see Miro every three or four weeks? He's been on AEW for quite some time now and yet he's been barely on TV besides that time he was TNT Champion. This is a good example of a great performer ruined by lazy booking. If TK is not interested in the man he should just let him go."
    arrancar wrote on 31.08.2023:
    [6.0] "When he's not taking long periods of time off due to injury, waning work ethic, or a lack of creative direction, Miro can be both a compelling character and an impressive brute powerhouse. After initially struggling in AEW with his awful "gamer" and "Best Man" gimmicks, he finally managed to the rediscover the same level of endearing charm as his previous "Bulgarian Brute" character with Rusev in WWE, this time with his "Redeemer" and "God's Favourite Champion" personas. These gimmicks may be rather goofy, but it's better to describe them as 'larger than life', since Miro made these characters really compelling because of the great conviction he displayed and clear joy he got when playing them. It's really cool how unafraid he is to lean into the more goofy side of things, which in turn makes those would-be goofy performances far more captivating because of how much life he brings to those roles. It of course also helps that he has a convincing look and really good offence to back this character work up, featuring some nicely impactful strikes and power moves, plus some submissions which look genuinely painful. Consistency is the real issue for Miro, since he'll go through periods of getting over with good performances, and then he'll go through long lay-offs where he's completely forgotten about, requiring him to need to restart the whole process of draw attention to himself and building himself up. It was nice to see him finally return to AEW in 2023 with the debut of Collision, but his in-ring/screen time during this period has still been rather inconsistent and minimal (3 squash matches in 2 whole months isn't exactly impressive, after all). Hopefully he now gets into the groove of more consistently having matches and contributing to ongoing feuds, which may lead to me upping his rating."
    KTG1515 wrote on 03.07.2023:
    [5.0] "I don't think there's any real major gripes with Rusev/Miro, but his time in AEW and WWE has been very interesting to me in any way."
    crs285 wrote on 04.04.2023:
    [9.0] "Why can nobody book Rusev/Miro properly. He is always over with the crowd and looks great in the ring but his booking never takes him to the main event. He can be the foreign monster heel or the beloved babyface. So much potential and it always seems wasted to me."
    Daigotsu wrote on 15.01.2023:
    [5.0] "Once he dropped that ridiculous gamer gimmick and became the Redeemer, Miro became pretty good. I think he's a little overrated, and I don't think he's a future champion or anything, but he wrestles a cool power style and his character is pretty cool, as long as he's sticking to backstage vignettes instead of actual in-ring promos. He seems to have lost a little size and it's hard to buy him as a "monster". It's hard to see if he's improved in the last year because he's basically never on TV."
    benh2 wrote on 11.01.2023:
    [5.0] "I don't know, Miro should be better than he is/was. A great look and a seriously good powerhouse frame but he's always flattered to deceive a bit. His initial US Champion run in WWE was probably his peak unfortunately. Bar the odd match here and there, it was all downhill after he entered WM31 in a tank. I haven't cared for his AEW run at all but he's certainly got a place as a upper midcard heel that babyfaces need to navigate on their way to the main event."
    spicyjuniormint wrote on 19.12.2022:
    [6.0] "A decent powerhouse but otherwise overrated, in my opinion. He? s far from terrible, but some people claim this guy is a main eventer and World Champion material and I just don? t see it. His in ring style is fun in short bursts but he? s not gonna blow you away bell to bell unless he has a great dance partner. His mic and character work are very one note. He has some solid charisma which has carried him this far, but I see him as a steady mid-card heel or tag team specialist that can occasionally challenge the world champ and give him a good fight before taking the L. There are dozens of guys who should be ahead of Miro in the line to the World Championship. People buying his stock in bulk need to let go of the past."
    TheOneAndOnlyCactus wrote on 13.09.2022:
    [8.0] "God's Favorite Champion is an amazing powerhouse, capable of being a lovable face or intimidating heel depending on the program he is working on. He has great strength and can hit hard. He seems to be considered a reliable hand in any company he works in, but it always feels like he could be more."
    GwenCube64 wrote on 06.09.2022:
    [7.0] "charismatic, solid promo, solid gimmicks, decent wrestler when not given too much restriction, a fan favorite for a reason"
    hoangminecraft6969 wrote on 21.07.2022:
    [7.0] "Good but not great wrestler, his streak in 2014 with the US title run was the peak of the Rusev gimmick (one of the best WWE gimmick in 2010s imo). Should have been WWE champion in 2017 instead of Jinder Mahal. I think his TNT title run is overrated and he's not really good in terms of ring skills compared to other AEW talents since it's hard to find any 4+ star match by him."
    zags7000 wrote on 09.05.2022:
    [6.0] "I always thought he was underrated in WWE and I still think he wasn't used properly. Rusev Day had so much potential and I believe they dropped the ball too early on that. However, I don't think he is as good as some believe either. He's ok, nothing more and nothing less. He's not bad but not great in the ring. He's better on the mic but not close to a top level guy. His promo's can be very strong though. Overall charismatically he can get people to react to him, which is good but I wouldn't say he's a top level guy. He's someone you use for depth in money in the bank or elimination chamber matches but you don't put him in to win. It was a rough start in AEW but his recent work before getting hurt was pretty strong and he shows he can deliver strong character work. He just lacks in too many other areas."
    macieggk wrote on 18.04.2022:
    [7.0] "I'm not a huge fan of Miro but he surely is a powerful heel, maybe not main event type of wrestler in my opinion, but a solid worker."
    PhenomenalGun wrote on 14.01.2022:
    [8.0] "Probably the best ex-WWE signing AEW has made since PAC, Miro pretty much has it all to become a main event player in AEW. He can wrestle a damn good powerhouse style, his strikes are on point, his finisher is great, his look is...well not as good as it used to be but besides the point, and his new character is solid. Only problem is his booking. I mean, Miro has a dominant run as TNT champion, managing to pull a decent match out of Fuego and starting the reign off with a bang against Darby, and then loses the title to Sammy Guevara in a bland match. He then replaces Mox against Danielson while he's working through an injury which resulted in a rather unspectacular match. Man, what a way to end off 2021 for Miro I guess, he pretty much lost all the momentum he had going to a guy who lost the title himself like a couple months after that to Trip-I mean Cody. Bonus point for being a fellow gamer/weeb though!"
    NikoKillbain wrote on 31.12.2021:
    [8.0] "Unlike a lot of recent AEW signings, Miro has proven his worth to his new company beyond any doubt, and it's mainly through the character he adds to their storyline universe. Even before the whole 'Miro vs God' angle, he proved his versatility in the Arcade Anarchy match, looking like an unstoppable beast while also being fun and engaging. Him climbing on Sue's car saying 'Hi Mom! ' is unforgettable. His TNT title run started well against Darby, picked up vs Eddie Kingston, then peaked beautifully vs Sammy Guevara."
    SrHunter wrote on 11.10.2021:
    [6.0] "I think Miro is a pretty solid overall, but he depends too much of his pponents to deliver good matches, i like his work as heel though."
    Remi wrote on 20.09.2021:
    "He has a good run as TNT champ. I didn't like him when he was in WWE but in AEW I think he is a very good heel. His appearance and shape is much better now but I'm not a big fan of him but he is good."
    MrIslamy wrote on 15.09.2021:
    [9.0] "Didn't like a single thing about him during his WWE run but in AEW everything he does is really good. He has a lot of charisma and is really good on the mic, he has a really interesting character, and he is good in the ring when he has a good opponent."
    Resident wrote on 06.08.2021:
    [9.0] "Great wrestler. One of the best in AEW and the current AEW TNT Champion. I thought he was great in WWE as Rusev, but under utilized. He is starting to achieve his potential in AEW. He has a lot of personality and charisma. A lot of power as well and seems difficult to be taken down."
    blastostg wrote on 28.07.2021:
    [3.0] "Inexplicably labeled as a hoss wrestler. He looks like a million bucks but hits like a million pillows. The absolute definition of a Fives Moves of Doom, WWE style wrestler. Does not fit in with AEW's roster at all. His promo ability is bad as well. Baffled at not only AEW signing him but putting a belt on him."
    Rocky7 wrote on 01.07.2021:
    [8.0] "I largely enjoyed Rusev during his WWE run, even though he had his ups and downs, especially with management, and although his AEW run didn't start on the best note with him being brought in as Kip Sabian's best friend for some reason, Miro has caught fire now, ditching the "Best Man" tag, now going by "The Redeemer" and "God's Favourite Champion" in his current reign with the TNT Championship. He's been a beast since winning the title, and his promos are really good and believable, which is crazy considering that English isn't his first language."
    LucaGG18 wrote on 25.05.2021:
    [7.0] "Like many people, I discovered this wrestler in the WWE. And like surely many people, I started to like him from the arrival of "Rusev Day". For my part, I find that Rusev was a wrestler rather badly exploited in the WWE, certainly he was a good US champion, but that's all there is to remember about him. Even his team with Aiden English never had a chance to shine, when they could have been good tag team champions. Fortunately, his arrival in AEW, and his victory over Darby Allin to become TNT Champion is starting to bring back the threat that we haven't seen in a while."
    Wrestlefan20 wrote on 23.05.2021:
    [5.0] "It's unfortunate how much he's declined since joining AEW. A poor debut, a bad look, bad booking, a bad character and ring rust he still hasn't shaken off have devalued him in a lot of people's eyes."
    Kung wrote on 22.05.2021:
    [7.0] "There's a lot to like about Miro; he looks like an absolute monster, he's got a great finisher, and he's also super charismatic. However, he seems like just another guy over at AEW. With that being said, he just recently won the TNT title, so we'll see what he does with it."
    Ma Stump Puller wrote on 15.03.2021:
    [5.0] "It's hard to really judge Miro/Rusev. On the one hand, guy's jacked and has a good look, he's not terrible in ring and he's got some good looking power moves. On the other, guy has no real charisma and his promo skills are basically next to zero. His highlights in terms of match quality are having a decent match with Cena a few times, a plodding Reigns feud, and facing Undertaker in a nothing bout. Sure he had good matches with Nakamura but.... well it's Nakamura lol, even him wrestling his B-show, couldn't care less style of wrestling is still miles beyond most wrestlers there. People said he was underutilised in the WWE but he's done exactly jackshit in AEW and has a terrible gimmick to boot. Guy should be a hoss worker instead of trying some bad niche work. To me, I don't see the hype. He's a good worker and could probably go a fair bit with some solid old school booking but at present he's just not impressing with his ring quality."
    StrongStyle2020 wrote on 05.03.2021:
    [6.0] "Still spinning his wheels at the moment, after the buzz his debut created. Starting to lose faith he will ever achieve the potential he has. One of the most underutilised and underachieving talents in AEW currently. Hoping he will leave his uninspiring videogame faction and kickstart his singles career again, but the longer this goes on, the more damage is done."
    Brett1980 wrote on 15.01.2021:
    [5.0] "Hopefully he will improve in AEW but to me he is the same Rusev. I do not remember a match where he stood out as great and he was given enough opportunities in WWE."
    Allout wrote on 21.12.2020:
    [8.0] "After seeing him on AEW i really was happy. He is considered to be the guy you want as main eventer but he is 6 in wrestling 6 in mic but has charisma that put him in 8 category and i know people will be happy if they made him their main event guy so yeah that's what you need but currently he isn't booked like that. 8 for now if future is good then it will stay like this or get lesser not more than this"
